updates.summary: This is a low-risk patch release focused entirely on bug fixes with no breaking changes. The fixes address a wide range of edge cases including color handling, selector parsing, CSS nesting, Sass preprocessor integration, and platform-specific font rendering. Upgrading is strongly recommended as it resolves real-world issues that may be affecting your build pipeline or rendered output.
ramifications: Arbitrary hex color canonicalization is now case-insensitive (e.g., `bg-[#FFF]` and `bg-[#fff]` now both resolve to `bg-white`), which may subtly alter generated class names in edge cases.
Windows users will see a change in default font stack from `system-ui`/`ui-sans-serif` to explicit platform fonts, which may affect CJK text rendering and visual appearance.
Achromatic theme colors mixed in polar color spaces like `oklch` will no longer shift hue, potentially changing the appearance of mixed colors in CSS output.
`--spacing(0)` now outputs `0px` instead of `0`, ensuring correct `<length>` type in `calc()` expressions, which may affect spacing calculations in edge cases.

The `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin will now correctly trigger rebuilds when Sass or other preprocessors modify the CSS without changing the source file on disk, potentially increasing build frequency in affected setups.
Minor type definition corrections or additions may slightly alter TypeScript compilation behavior
Any newly added or corrected type definitions could surface previously hidden type errors in existing code
No runtime impact as this is a types-only package

updates.summary: This is a low-risk patch release containing five targeted bug fixes across the auth, functions, storage, and core supabase modules. The changes address noise reduction in error logging, resource cleanup for fetch abort listeners, case-insensitive Content-Type matching, URL encoding for CDN purge methods, and a Deno compatibility fix. No breaking changes are introduced and the fixes are narrowly scoped to improve correctness and stability.
ramifications: Auth aborted/transient fetch failures will now log at warn level instead of error, which may reduce noise in production logging pipelines but could affect any alerting rules keyed on console.error output.
The functions invoke() method now properly cleans up cross-signal abort listeners on return, preventing potential memory leaks in long-running applications that invoke Edge Functions frequently.
Content-Type header matching in functions responses is now case-insensitive, which may change behavior when servers return non-standard casing (e.g., 'content-type' vs 'Content-Type').
Storage CDN purge methods now URL-encode object keys, which fixes a bug but could change behavior if previously relying on unencoded paths in CDN purge operations.
Deno users will no longer see spurious Node.js warnings, improving compatibility in Deno-based deployments.

updates.summary: This is a low-risk patch release focused entirely on bug fixes with no breaking changes. The fixes address a wide range of edge cases including color handling, selector parsing, CSS nesting, Sass preprocessor integration, and platform-specific font rendering. Upgrading is strongly recommended as it resolves real-world issues that may be affecting your build pipeline or rendered output.
ramifications: Arbitrary hex color canonicalization is now case-insensitive (e.g., `bg-[#FFF]` and `bg-[#fff]` now both resolve to `bg-white`), which may subtly alter generated class names in edge cases.
Windows users will see a change in default font stack from `system-ui`/`ui-sans-serif` to explicit platform fonts, which may affect CJK text rendering and visual appearance.
Achromatic theme colors mixed in polar color spaces like `oklch` will no longer shift hue, potentially changing the appearance of mixed colors in CSS output.
`--spacing(0)` now outputs `0px` instead of `0`, ensuring correct `<length>` type in `calc()` expressions, which may affect spacing calculations in edge cases.

The `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin will now correctly trigger rebuilds when Sass or other preprocessors modify the CSS without changing the source file on disk, potentially increasing build frequency in affected setups.
Minor type definition corrections or additions may slightly alter TypeScript compilation behavior
Any newly added or corrected type definitions could surface previously hidden type errors in existing code
No runtime impact as this is a types-only package

updates.summary: This is a pure bug-fix patch release addressing 15 distinct issues across the tailwindcss ecosystem including CLI, PostCSS, Vite, and browser integrations. The fixes cover edge cases in color handling, selector parsing, CSS nesting, spacing optimization, and font rendering for CJK text. No breaking changes are introduced and the fixes are broadly beneficial, reducing incorrect or inconsistent behavior.
ramifications: Font stack change (using explicit platform fonts instead of system-ui/ui-sans-serif) may cause subtle visual differences in CJK text rendering on Windows, though this is an intentional improvement.
The fix for achromatic theme colors preventing hue shifts in polar color spaces like oklch may slightly alter rendered colors if your design relied on the previous (incorrect) behavior.

The bg-[#fff] / bg-[#FFF] canonicalization fix means hex color classes are now case-insensitively matched to theme colors, which could reduce duplicate CSS output but is unlikely to cause visual regressions.
The selector parsing fix for patterns like [data-foo]div changes how such selectors are interpreted, which could affect styling if your project uses such adjacent attribute+element selectors.
The --spacing(0) optimization now outputs 0px instead of 0, which is more correct for calc() contexts but could affect tests or snapshots that assert on the exact CSS output.
